title: Lord Grey de Ruthyn to Griffith ap David ap Griffith
summary: The checked-in XML is a repository copy of University of Michigan CME00124, which encodes Francis Charles Hingeston's Letter XVI on printed pp.35-38 of his 1860 Royal and Historical Letters, volume I. Hingeston directly transcribed Grey's contemporary paper answer-copy pasted at British Library Cotton MS Cleopatra F III, f.70b. For supporting passages from Gruffudd's 11 June letter at f.72 and Grey's 23 June report at f.70, however, Hingeston explicitly reprinted Henry Ellis's 1827 transcriptions, so those paths run through Ellis rather than directly to the codex. Complete copies of both nineteenth-century volumes and Norman Davis's supporting 1967 article are cached with fixity. No public manuscript facsimile was found in the current British Library record; physical consultation and paid whole-object photography remain request routes.
review_notes: The primary subject is the repository artifact at CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00124.xml; the U-M encoding, abstract historical letter, Hingeston and Ellis editions, manuscript, scans, catalog description, and Davis article are separate entities.
The cataloged historical work is Grey's reply, not the whole three-letter dossier. Hingeston's two groups of Gruffudd excerpts and one Grey-to-Prince excerpt are supporting content parts.

Hingeston calls the answer-copy a contemporary paper manuscript pasted on f.70b, while the current British Library material inventory calls f.70 parchment. The attached document's carrier and the host leaf are modeled as separate physical layers, so the statements are not treated as contradictory.
Hingeston's main text is directly related to f.70b. His supporting excerpts are passage-level reprints from Ellis, who directly transcribed f.70 and f.72.

The current British Library catalog's Digitised Content field is blank and no viewer link was found as of 2026-07-13. This dated negative finding does not prove that no internal, unpublished, or legacy photography exists.
The pinned CME root LICENSE uses Unlicense language framed as software. Its application to corpus XML/data/markup remains unresolved and is not broadened into a high-confidence rights claim.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00124
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00124; the file is 10,747 bytes.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00124
entities.description: The keyed electronic text of Hingeston's Letter XVI, including the main reply, editorial heading and notes, two selected Gruffudd excerpt groups, and the final Grey-to-Prince excerpt.

entities.label: Reginald Grey's reply to Gruffudd ap Dafydd ap Gruffudd
alternate_labels: Lord Grey de Ruthyn to Griffith ap David ap Griffith
entities.description: A Middle English political letter written after 11 June and no later than 23 June 1400. Grey recapitulates Gruffudd's complaints, denies plans to attack loyal subjects, invokes royal law, and threatens hanging in a closing passage containing seven lines of rhyming doggerel.

date_statements.label: Composition
date_statements.value: after 11 June and no later than 23 June 1400
date_statements.notes: The lower bound is Gruffudd's initiating letter; the upper bound is Grey's dated report that he had sent the answer and enclosed a copy.

entities.label: Francis Charles Hingeston, Royal and Historical Letters, volume I (1860)
entities.description: The immediate source edition for CME00124. Letter XVI prints Grey's reply on pp.35-38 from Cotton Cleopatra F III, f.70b, with extensive notes reconstructing the three-letter sequence and selected passages reprinted from Ellis.

bibliographic.citation: Francis Charles Hingeston, ed., Royal and Historical Letters during the Reign of Henry the Fourth, King of England and of France, and Lord of Ireland, vol. I, A.D. 1399-1404, Rerum Britannicarum Medii Aevi Scriptores 18 (London: Longman, Green, Longman, and Roberts, 1860), xxii-xxiii, 35-38.
bibliographic.title: Royal and Historical Letters during the Reign of Henry the Fourth, King of England and of France, and Lord of Ireland

notes: The preface says the volume collects letters of historical interest not already published in the principal earlier collections and draws from Cotton, Harley, Public Record Office, Bodleian, and episcopal sources.
Hingeston's headnote reads: 'MS. Cotton. Cleop. F. III. fol. 70 b.-On paper; a contemporaneous MS.'

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Hingeston volume I (1860)
entities.description: A complete 596-page scan of the source volume. The item occupies PDF pages 155-158 and Internet Archive leaves n154-n157; the relevant preface discussion is at PDF pages 30-31.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete-volume PDF with OCR and provider metadata derivatives

entities.label: British Library, Cotton MS Cleopatra F III
entities.description: A 213-folio collection of Privy Council and royal-administrative materials, much concerning the Welsh rebellion and the 1415 French expedition. The current whole-record title broadly calls the materials 'all originals,' but Hingeston's component-level evidence identifies the target at f.70b as a contemporary answer-copy; that component identification controls here. Grey's 23 June report is at f.70 and Gruffudd's 11 June letter at f.72.

date_statements.label: Codex materials
date_statements.value: last quarter of the fourteenth century to first quarter of the fifteenth century

holding.notes: All manuscripts and archives are consulted at the British Library in London; current physical requests require a valid Reader Pass and the exact shelfmark.

physical_description.support: Mixed parchment and paper; the target is a paper document attached to the verso of parchment host f.70, while f.72 lies in a paper run
physical_description.extent: 213 folios: 2 early modern endleaves plus 211 further folios; target copy at f.70b, related report at f.70, and initiating letter at f.72
notes: Leaves were damaged by fire in 1731.
The present binding is British Museum, 1961.
Hingeston's paper-support statement describes the attached answer-copy; the current catalog's parchment statement describes its host f.70.

entities.label: British Library Archives and Manuscripts record 040-001103831
entities.description: The current official whole-codex record for Cotton MS Cleopatra F III, with identifiers, collection context, dates, languages, physical inventory, fire-damage note, provenance, origin, and access route. It does not enumerate the target letters individually and exposes no digitised-content link.

entities.label: Henry Ellis, Original Letters, Second Series, volume I (1827)
entities.description: Ellis directly prints Grey's 23 June report from Cotton Cleopatra F III, f.70, as Letter I on pp.1-4 and Gruffudd's 11 June letter from f.72 as Letter II on pp.5-7. Hingeston explicitly reprints selected passages from these two edition items.

bibliographic.citation: Henry Ellis, Original Letters, Illustrative of English History; Including Numerous Royal Letters: From Autographs in the British Museum, and One or Two Other Collections, Second Series, 4 vols., vol. I (London: Harding and Lepard, 1827), 1-7.
bibliographic.title: Original Letters, Illustrative of English History; Including Numerous Royal Letters: From Autographs in the British Museum, and One or Two Other Collections

entities.label: Wikimedia Commons complete PDF of Ellis, Second Series, volume I (1827)
entities.description: A complete 373-page scan of Ellis's volume, derived from Internet Archive item dli.granth.86772 and currently distributed by Wikimedia Commons. Letters I-II occupy PDF pages 20-26.
notes: The source Internet Archive item is currently dark, but the complete Commons copy remains publicly downloadable.

entities.label: Norman Davis, 'Style and Stereotype in Early English Letters' (1967)
entities.description: A scholarly article on convention and colloquial language in early English letters. At p.17 note 49 Davis identifies Grey's seven closing lines as intentional occasional rhyming doggerel and cites Hingeston pp.xxiii and 38.

bibliographic.citation: Norman Davis, 'Style and Stereotype in Early English Letters,' Leeds Studies in English, new series 1 (1967): 7-17.
bibliographic.title: Style and Stereotype in Early English Letters

entities.label: University of Leeds PDF of Davis's 1967 article
entities.description: The complete twelve-page PDF supplied by the University of Leeds digital library, consisting of one cover sheet and the eleven-page article; the relevant note is on article p.17 / PDF page 12.
notes: The PDF is readable, but qpdf reports inconsistent linearization hint-table/shared-object entries and exits 3 after completing the check.

subject.description: Letter I, Grey to the Prince of Wales, dated 23 June 1400
Letter II, Gruffudd to Grey, dated 11 June 1400

object.description: Original Gruffudd letter; exact recto/verso subdivision was not established
Complete published Second Series volume I

A supporting form relationship, not an upstream transmission path.

access.access_method: Request the exact shelfmark with a valid Reader Pass; consult manuscripts and archives in London
notes: The catalog says readers whose passes were issued or renewed since 21 March 2024 may request items in advance through the form; older passes must be renewed. Item availability and handling restrictions remain subject to staff review.

access.access_method: British Library digitisation-services order form; quote and condition assessment required

local_copies.label: British Library digitisation rate card 2025/26
notes: The rate card renders successfully, but qpdf --check exits 3 with nonfatal linearization warnings concerning uncompressed object ordering, a page-0 shared-identifier/hint-table mismatch, and an incorrect outlines-table offset.

access.contact: permissions@bl.uk for image reuse permission; licensing@bl.uk for licensing enquiries
access.format: Reference JPEG, reproduction JPEG/TIFF, or bespoke photography as approved
notes: For whole-object coverage, specify boards/covers, spine, pastedowns, both early modern endleaves, every folio recto and verso including blanks, stubs, repairs and detached matter, and edges when offered; identify f.70b as the target and f.70/f.72 as supporting loci.

At the published 2025/26 reproduction-TIFF rate of £27.70 for the first image plus £7.25 each subsequent image, 426 leaf sides alone would have a mechanical baseline of £3,108.95. This is not a quote and excludes covers, endleaves beyond the 213-folio count, special handling, bespoke work, and any rights fees.
Because the codex was fire-damaged and consists of original manuscript documents, the Library may decline, limit, or specially quote photography after conservation assessment.

rights.component: Modern XML transcription, structure, and metadata
rights.basis: The pinned repository root LICENSE contains Unlicense text framed as software, but no path-specific statement establishes that it intentionally covers corpus XML/data/markup.
notes: Do not treat this caution about the modern XML layer as restricting the underlying medieval letter.

rights.component: University of Michigan keyed electronic text and presentation
rights.basis: The current CME collection identifies its public-domain source editions and presents No Copyright - United States / NoC-US framing; this is a provider rights statement, not an open license for every modern layer.
The letter was created in 1400 and published in a nineteenth-century edition; any U.S. copyright term has expired.
The edition was published in 1860, before 1930, and is in the U.S. public domain.

rights.component: Exact Internet Archive scan and derivatives
rights.basis: The inspected item metadata contains no licenseurl or rights value; public download availability alone does not establish a redistribution license for the exact scan files.
Ownership, access, and handling of the physical object are distinct from copyright in its medieval text or in newly made photographs.
The current catalog footer identifies catalog content under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 except where otherwise stated.
notes: This statement concerns descriptive catalog content, not the physical manuscript, underlying medieval texts, API structure, or newly supplied images.

rights.component: New reference or reproduction images supplied by British Library Imaging Services
rights.basis: The imaging terms distinguish reference and reproduction images and require written permission before supplied images are copied or reproduced; availability, fees, and permitted use are assessed per order.
notes: Private research delivery does not itself authorize posting the images in this repository. Obtain written permission for the exact intended reuse.

rights.basis: The edition was published in 1827 and is in the U.S. public domain.
The exact Commons file page marks the work public domain in the source country and United States and applies Public Domain Mark 1.0.
notes: Public Domain Mark is a status label, not a license.

rights.basis: The article was published in 1967 and no explicit open license was found on the inspected issue landing page or PDF.

rights.component: Exact University of Leeds article PDF
rights.basis: Public download access was confirmed, but no explicit license authorizing redistribution of the exact PDF was found.
notes: Local private research caching does not establish permission to redistribute the PDF.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Three related manuscript letters and their documentary relationship
editorial_practices.representation: One directly transcribed reply with selected supporting passages from Ellis and an editorial reconstruction of order
editorial_practices.description: Hingeston prints Grey's reply in full but supplies only selected excerpts from Gruffudd's initiating letter and Grey's enclosing report. The supporting excerpts are explicitly mediated through Ellis.
notes: Hingeston omits at least Gruffudd's salutation and one sentence between the two excerpt runs.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Cotton Cleopatra F III letters concerning the June 1400 conflict
editorial_practices.representation: Two complete letter items with historical notes
editorial_practices.description: Ellis prints Grey's report as Letter I and Gruffudd's complaint as Letter II but does not print Grey's reply at f.70b, which Hingeston later supplies.
editorial_practices.scope: Second Series volume I, printed pp.1-7

editorial_practices.source_feature: Hingeston Letter XVI in a complete printed volume
editorial_practices.representation: A standalone keyed XML item containing printed pp.35-38
editorial_practices.description: The U-M item extracts and encodes Letter XVI with its notes rather than the complete Hingeston volume; page breaks preserve the printed-page boundaries.

open_questions.question: Where exactly was Grey's reply composed, and does the f.70b paper preserve an address, dateline, endorsement, or omitted text that would answer this?
next_steps: Inspect or obtain complete color photography of f.70 recto and verso, the attached paper, and adjacent surfaces.
Do not substitute Ruthin, the dateline of the separate enclosing report, for the reply's unstated composition place.

open_questions.question: What are the precise dimensions, attachment method, orientation, and current foliation of the paper answer-copy mounted on parchment host f.70?
next_steps: Ask British Library staff for condition and cataloging detail before ordering.
Request overall recto/verso and attachment-detail photography if conservation policy permits.

open_questions.question: Does any public or unpublished complete facsimile of Cotton MS Cleopatra F III exist beyond the blank current digitised-content field?
next_steps: Ask British Library Reference Services about internal, legacy, microfilm, or unpublished photography.
If none exists, seek a quote for whole-object color photography and preserve the exact f.70b/f.70/f.72 work mapping.

open_questions.question: Does the CME repository's software-worded root Unlicense intentionally apply to XML markup and data?
next_steps: Request a path-specific clarification from the repository maintainer.
Until clarified, preserve copyright status as unknown for the modern XML layer and do not broaden the software wording.
